scope: DeepSeek-V4 shared latent-KV sparse MLA backward compressed-prefix dKV accumulation

commands.s128_mixed: HIP_VISIBLE_DEVICES=0 CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=0 python3 benchmark_sparse_mla_shared_kv_compressed_dkv_bmm_floor.py --seq-len 128 --batch 1 --heads 64 --dim 512 --compress-ratio 4 --topk 512 --dtype bf16 --math-dtype fp32 --bmm-input-dtype bf16 --accum-mode flat_gemm --warmup-iters 1 --timed-iters 3 --json
commands.s128_bf16: HIP_VISIBLE_DEVICES=0 CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=0 python3 benchmark_sparse_mla_shared_kv_compressed_dkv_bmm_floor.py --seq-len 128 --batch 1 --heads 64 --dim 512 --compress-ratio 4 --topk 512 --dtype bf16 --math-dtype bf16 --bmm-input-dtype bf16 --accum-mode flat_gemm --warmup-iters 1 --timed-iters 3 --json
commands.s4096_mixed: HIP_VISIBLE_DEVICES=0 CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=0 python3 benchmark_sparse_mla_shared_kv_compressed_dkv_bmm_floor.py --seq-len 4096 --batch 1 --heads 64 --dim 512 --compress-ratio 4 --topk 512 --dtype bf16 --math-dtype fp32 --bmm-input-dtype bf16 --accum-mode flat_gemm --warmup-iters 1 --timed-iters 5 --skip-correctness --skip-reference-timing --json
commands.s4096_bf16: HIP_VISIBLE_DEVICES=0 CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=0 python3 benchmark_sparse_mla_shared_kv_compressed_dkv_bmm_floor.py --seq-len 4096 --batch 1 --heads 64 --dim 512 --compress-ratio 4 --topk 512 --dtype bf16 --math-dtype bf16 --bmm-input-dtype bf16 --accum-mode flat_gemm --warmup-iters 1 --timed-iters 5 --skip-correctness --skip-reference-timing --json

read: Flattening heads and sequence into one HS row dimension gives a viable MFMA-friendly compressed dKV floor but does not materially beat the head-batched BMM mixed floor. Full S4096 mixed flat-GEMM is 7.05 ms versus 7.07 ms for BMM, with peak memory 5.60 GiB versus 5.85 GiB. Chunk1024/chunk512 flat-GEMM are 7.69/7.78 ms and 3.73/3.42 GiB, slightly lower memory than equivalent BMM chunks and less bend at chunk512. The useful new signal is pure BF16: S128 flat-GEMM passes the 0.2 tolerance at max diff 0.198, where the earlier BF16 BMM point missed by 0.001, and S4096 BF16 flat-GEMM is 4.73 ms with 4.10 GiB peak. The production path should still be a fused streaming MFMA compressed-dKV kernel; flat-GEMM mainly clarifies that direct head reduction and BF16 accumulation are viable design choices.
